what_it_does: Scopes a thin-slice plan to ingest Tim's theorem from the OVERLORD substrate into the COMPANY recall corpus so the company can recall its own base, deliberately deferring mass embedding until the approach is greenlit.
observations: Tim's theorem is already indexed in the OVERLORD substrate (8,629 files / 116,712 chunks across 5 vaults) but the COMPANY corpus is currently code-only.
The proposed mechanics reuse existing company paths: walk_files → cognition.run_items → embed_corpus_to_spaces → company FsStore, with recall via recall_for_decision / corpus(op=query).
Two source options are listed: reading vault markdown directly or pulling already-chunked, axis/face/articulation-tagged text from the overlord substrate.db.
The plan insists on a thin-slice proof first: the tims_verbatim catalog plus the 189-chunk formal package in relative-difference, embedded into one company space and verified by use.
Four open decisions are explicitly called out before execution: which space, curate vs full, source pull method, and refresh cadence.
Embedding is flagged as contending with the live :8007 service, so full 116k embedding must wait for a quiet window.
Status is scoped and ready but not executed, awaiting greenlight especially on decisions A and B.
standouts: Applies the lead's own thin-slice discipline to a foundational ingest task.
Identifies a tiny high-value unit (~189 chunks + tims_verbatim) as the proof target rather than all 116k chunks.
Proposes a dedicated theorem/base space to make 'recall the base' addressable and avoid diluting worldview/principles.
Frames verification as a use test: recall_for_decision on an axes/invariant decision must surface Tim's words.
conventions: References concrete runtime symbols and file paths (runtime/corpus.py:walk_files, runtime/cognition.py:embed_corpus_to_spaces, recall_for_decision).
Separates scoped plan from execution and marks open decisions explicitly.
Uses issue/task identifiers (#65, g/t-1781885694, 3475aa6) and substrate commit references.
concerns: Depends on cross-system plumbing to read the overlord substrate.db from Windows-path vaults.
Embedding all 116k chunks risks burying the theorem in noise and consuming heavy resources during live :8007 use.
Key design decisions (space choice, curation scope) are unresolved, so the file is a blocker rather than a ready recipe.
No explicit rollback or deduplication strategy if the theorem content is later updated.
notes: A scoped, decision-gated plan to move Tim's theorem from the overlord substrate into company recall, starting with a tiny verifiable slice rather than a full 116k-chunk embed.
questions: Where exactly are the overlord substrate.db and vault roots located, and are they reachable from the company runtime?
What is the schema and content of the tims_verbatim catalog?
Would creating a new company space require schema or index changes beyond the existing embed_corpus_to_spaces path?
Who is Tim and what is the theorem's core statement in plain terms?
What does #65 refer to, and what does 'recall its own base' mean operationally for downstream decisions?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it scopes a thin-slice ingest plan and deliberately does not execute mass embedding.
apparent_themes: grounding a system in its own foundational base
corpus hygiene and avoiding signal dilution
intention_signals: The file is trying to get the company recall system to retrieve Tim's theorem as foundational grounding, using the smallest possible verifiable ingest to prove the loop before scaling.
novelty: Treats a personal theorem as a recall substrate/base rather than code, and plans cross-substrate ingestion from an overlord vault into the company corpus while preserving axis/face/articulation structural tags.
contribution: A scoped thin-slice plan, a curated high-value chunk set, an explicit decision framework, and a verify-by-use method for making foundational theorem content available to company recall.
summary_for_embedding: A scoped plan to ingest Tim's theorem from the OVERLORD substrate into the COMPANY recall corpus via a small verifiable slice, reusing existing embed paths and deferring full-scale embedding until greenlit.
intention_for_embedding: To make the company system able to recall its own foundational theorem base by embedding a curated subset into a company corpus space and proving retrieval by use.
